page_content:
SOCY456 Smart Machines and Human Prospects (3 Credits)

Artificial intelligence is everywhere and never sleeps. It is transforming our social institutions in intended and unintended ways. While scientists debate the feasibility of engineering conscious machines with general intelligence, no one debates that the global race is on to create

more potent computers. Through targeted research, discussion, and presentation of findings students will answer a specific question on how, where, and in what ways society is being changed by smart machines. Prerequisite: SOCY201 and SOCY202; or permission of the Sociology department.

Credit Only Granted for. SOCY416 or SOCY456.
